file: 903_Samikannu 等 - 2020 - Highly dispersed NbOPO4SBA-15 as a versatile acid catalyst upon production of renewable jet-fuel fr.txt

experiments.description: 在无溶剂条件下，使用NbOPO₄/SBA-15等固体酸催化剂催化2-甲基呋喃与各种羰基化合物（丁醛、糠醛、丙酮、环己酮）进行C-C偶联反应。
experiments.text_snippet: The solventless HAA of 2-methylfuran with different aldehydes and ketones were carried out in 5ml micro batch reactors placed on a heating block maintained at the desired reaction temperatures (40−60°C). In a typical experiment, 50mg of catalyst, 5.63mmol of 2-methyl furan and 2.5mmol of the carbonyl compound (butanal, furfural, acetone or cyclohexanone) were loaded into the glass reactor and magnetically stirred (500rpm) at the desired temperature for 0−5h.

experiments.description: 使用Pd/NbOPO₄/SBA-15等双功能催化剂，在高压氢气条件下，将HAA反应得到的呋喃甲烷含氧物加氢脱氧，转化为喷气燃料范围的C9-C16烃类。
experiments.text_snippet: In the hydrodeoxygenation step, the furanic coupling products were further converted to produce jet fuel-range branched hydrocarbons using the metal (Pd) loaded catalysts. All catalytic experiments were performed in batch mode using a ∼14mL mini autoclave... In a typical reaction, 50mg catalyst, 1.25mmol of the HAA product and 3ml cyclohexane (solvent) were loaded into the autoclave... pressurized to reach the desired H₂ pressure (typically 50bar), finally the reaction mixture was heated to the required temperature (200−300°C) for the reaction period of 4−12h.

Catalyst stability.original: Stable for ~65 h continuous operation, maintain high activity upon reuse after calcination

In a typical experiment, 50mg of catalyst, 5.63mmol of 2-methyl furan and 2.5mmol of the carbony...

Feedstock.original: HAA reaction products (furanic coupling products, e.g., 2-methyl-5-(1-(5-methylfuran-2-yl)butyl)furan)

Product Selectivity.original: Varies with catalyst; high selectivity to jet-fuel range (C9-C14) hydrocarbons over Pd/Nb₂O₅/SBA-15 and Pd/NbOPO₄/SBA-15 catalysts with weak-to-medium acid sites

Catalyst stability.original: Stable upon reuse (Pd/NbP-B/SBA-15 showed consistent activity and selectivity)
